pokemon:cutiefly.name: Cutiefly
pokemon:cutiefly.summary: Myriads of Cutiefly flutter above the heads of people who have auras resembling those of flowers.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Myriads of Cutiefly flutter above the heads of people who have auras resembling those of flowers.
An opponent’s aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent’s next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.
It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it’s able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.
Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur’s pollen.
Cutiefly can pick up the auras of living things. It skillfully reads those auras to predict its foes’ movements and make fools of them.
Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. In fields of flowers, it gets into skirmishes with Butterfree over food.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Myriads of Cutiefly flutter above the heads of people who have auras resembling those of flowers.

pokemon:ribombee.name: Ribombee
pokemon:ribombee.summary: Some of Ribombee’s pollen puffs are highly nutritious. They are sometimes sold as supplements.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Some of Ribombee’s pollen puffs are highly nutritious. They are sometimes sold as supplements.
Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.
It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.
It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs’ effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.
It can predict the weather from moisture levels and wind direction. Ribombee only reveals itself when there are a few clear days in a row.
Rain makes pollen damp, so Ribombee hates rain. When it sees ominous clouds, it finds a hollow in a tree, where it waits stock-still.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Some of Ribombee’s pollen puffs are highly nutritious. They are sometimes sold as supplements.
